title: Help troubleshooting overview
text: Troubleshooting means checking the system in a simple order: what the user asked, what block should handle it, whether the block is enabled, whether runtime files changed, and whether logs show an error.
short_answer: Troubleshooting is checking the request, the block, runtime files, and logs in order.
examples: HOW DO I DEBUG THE APP?
checklist: Check if the block is enabled.
Run a small test script if available.

title: Assistant did not answer
text: If the assistant did not answer, check whether the input reached the app, whether the decision layer produced a task, whether the LLM generated text, and whether output files were written.
short_answer: Check input, decision, LLM response, and output files.
examples: WHY DID THE ASSISTANT NOT ANSWER?
checklist: Check latest input or transcript file.
If TTS is involved, check latest_tts.json and tts_playback.json.

title: Memory not found
text: If memory is not found, check whether the item was saved in the right space, whether the query uses similar words, whether the memory backend is active, and whether fallback storage has records.
short_answer: Check the right space, query words, backend status, and fallback records.
checklist: Confirm if the memory was manual or inferred.
Check the correct user space: notes, rules, prompts, projects, or help.
Search using words that exist in the saved item.
If using Mem0 or Qdrant, confirm the service is running.
Run a memory smoke test if available.

title: Command not recognized
text: If a command is not recognized, check whether the command phrase exists in help examples, aliases, or command matcher patterns. Voice commands often need extra aliases because speech transcripts may not be exact.
short_answer: Check aliases, command patterns, and speech transcript wording.
checklist: Check if the command has aliases.
Check if the command matcher expects a different phrase.
Add common speech variants if needed.
Test the command with a short direct phrase.

title: Help answer is weak
text: If a help answer is weak, the help item probably needs a better short answer, clearer tags, more aliases, related topics, and real examples.
short_answer: Improve the help item with better text, tags, aliases, related topics, and examples.
